content_markdown:
The graph shows the variation of pressure $P$ with volume $V$ of an ideal gas during a thermodynamic cycle.

During which stages is work done on the gas and work done by the gas?

diagram_metadata.description: A P-V diagram showing a triangular cycle with vertices X, Y, and Z. The process X to Y is an expansion (downward curve/slope), Y to Z is a compression at constant pressure (horizontal line to the left), and Z to X is an isochoric pressure increase (vertical line upwards).

options.label: A
options.text: Work done on gas: $X \rightarrow Y$ only; Work done by gas: $Y \rightarrow Z$ only

options.label: B
options.text: Work done on gas: $X \rightarrow Y$ and $Z \rightarrow X$; Work done by gas: $Y \rightarrow Z$ only

options.label: C
options.text: Work done on gas: $Y \rightarrow Z$ only; Work done by gas: $Z \rightarrow X$ and $X \rightarrow Y$

options.label: D
options.text: Work done on gas: $Y \rightarrow Z$ only; Work done by gas: $X \rightarrow Y$ only

vibe_explanation: Work is done by the gas when it expands (volume increases), which occurs during the stage $X \rightarrow Y$. Work is done on the gas when it is compressed (volume decreases), which occurs during the stage $Y \rightarrow Z$. In the stage $Z \rightarrow X$, the volume is constant (isochoric), so no work is done.
